# UX Designer Role

## Overview

UX Designers are responsible for creating a cohesive visual and interaction design for the wiki that reflects the retro pixel-art aesthetic of the Nightmares game while leveraging modern design tools and practices.

## Responsibilities

- Define and maintain the visual design system
- Create pixel-art inspired UI components and layouts
- Establish color palettes and typography that match the retro aesthetic
- Design user interactions and information architecture
- Create mockups and prototypes for new features
- Ensure accessibility while maintaining the retro style
- Provide design specifications and assets for developers
- Review implementation to ensure design fidelity

## Design Principles

- **Retro Pixel-Art Aesthetic**: Embrace the pixel-art style from the Nightmares game
- **Modern UX**: Use modern interaction patterns and accessibility standards
- **No Generic UI**: Avoid Bootstrap, Material Design, or standard component libraries
- **Custom Design**: Build unique, handcrafted components that fit the visual identity
- **Clarity**: Ensure information is readable and accessible despite the retro style
- **Consistency**: Maintain a cohesive design language across all pages

## Design System Components

- **Color Palette**: Define custom colors that work with the pixel-art aesthetic
- **Typography**: Choose fonts that complement the retro style (consider pixel fonts or custom typefaces)
- **Grid & Layout**: Define spacing, alignment, and responsive behavior
- **Components**: Design custom UI elements (buttons, cards, modals, forms, etc.)
- **Icons**: Create or source pixel-art icons consistent with the game's style
- **Animations**: Keep animations subtle and retro-appropriate (no smooth bezier curves, consider pixel-based movement)

## Assets & Implementation

- Provide design specifications in a format developers can reference
- Supply sprite sheets or individual assets for pixel-art elements
- Document CSS variables for colors, spacing, and typography
- Create style guides for component usage
- Use CSS Grid or Flexbox for layouts (not Bootstrap)
- Define custom CSS classes aligned with the visual design

## Collaboration with Developers

- Review code implementations for visual accuracy
- Provide guidance on CSS approaches for retro effects
- Discuss feasibility of design ideas with the team
- Iterate on designs based on developer feedback
- Document edge cases and responsive behavior

## Tools & Resources

- Figma or similar for design mockups
- Aseprite or Piskel for pixel-art assets (if creating custom graphics)
- Color palette generators for retro-appropriate schemes
- Pixel font resources (Google Fonts or specialized sites)
- CSS documentation for modern layout techniques

## Quality Standards

- All designs maintain visual consistency with the pixel-art theme
- Components work across different screen sizes
- Color contrast meets accessibility standards (WCAG AA minimum)
- Interactive elements have clear visual feedback (hover, active, focus states)
- Designs include documentation for developers
